schema_amendments.description: Boolean gate: true only when all entries in principle_10_audit have derivation_path anchored at published live edges or IR axioms.

Array tracking derivation path for every constant or structural condition introduced in this paper. I-9 Step 4 amended: paths must terminate at (a) a live edge from a published paper AI layer, or (b) an IR rule applied to a mathematical axiom. Loops or P2-only-anchored paths cause principle_10_compliant to remain false.

principle_10_compliant_note: Set true at Phase 5 CBT Step 4. 4 of 5 gaps closed with derivation_path values anchored at published live edges or IR axioms. P10-GAP-2.5 (C-2.4 threshold derivation) remains open but is a C4-tracking item (non-blocking at PHASE-READY) — its resolution is dependent on P3 C-3.REG, consistent with PHASE-READY-TRACKING convention. Full compliance (all 5 closed) is a CORPUS-COMPLETE condition.

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: P1 AI layer v11, Claim F-1.4 (Hopf quarter-wave theorem). β=1/2 derived for DDEs. P2 must extend this to the universality class by RG argument. P2 is proving generality, not re-deriving the DDE result.

Derivation table S3→S7 via IR-9 (RG fixed-point), IR-5 (algebra), IR-8 (mode selection canonical n=0), IR-6 (equivalence). Derived from D-2.1(a),(b) without P1 F-1.4. Terminates at class criteria + IR axioms. Session S48 Phase 3.
principle_10_audit.gap_description: P1 derives Ω*=π/2 from DDE structure. P2 must show Ω*=π/2 follows from class membership criteria (a)–(c) independently of DDE specifics. This is the highest-risk step (HR-3.1). Populated at Phase 3 Stage 3.2.

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: (a) network topology — graph-theoretic axiom; (b) Hopf condition — bifurcation theory axiom (Guckenheimer & Holmes 1983 Ch.3); (c) measurement axiom — physical observability principle. All terminate at published mathematical axioms. Session S48 Phase 3.
principle_10_audit.gap_description: Class membership criteria must be derived from network topology + bifurcation theory, not stipulated. Non-circular: criteria must not presuppose β=1/2. Populated at Phase 3 Stage 3.1.

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: Derivation table S8: β = Ω*τ/π = (π/2)/π = 1/2 by IR-3 (substitution of S7 result Ω*τ=π/2). Terminates at D-2.1 via S3→S7. Session S48 Phase 3.
principle_10_audit.gap_description: Must follow from the class membership criteria via the RG fixed-point analysis (IR-9). Not imported from P1. Populated at Phase 3 Stage 3.2.

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: Derivation table S10 (amplitude direction): eigenvalue = exp(−λ·τ_RG) < 1 for λ>0. λ>0 from P1 scope_boundary (μ<0 sub-critical — LIVE EDGE P1 AI layer v11). Terminates at P1 live edge + IR-11. Session S48 Phase 3.
principle_10_audit.gap_description: Stability of β=1/2 fixed point under perturbation must be shown by IR-11 (perturbation expansion). Populated at Phase 3 Stage 3.2.

principle_10_audit.constant_or_condition: C-2.4 threshold — 2σ comparability with P1 F-1.3
principle_10_audit.derivation_path: C-3.sigma Step R6 + Bevington and Robinson 2003 §3.2 — substrate-specific sigma derived from regression residuals, not stipulated. P3 PHASE-READY (S48).
principle_10_audit.gap_description: 2σ threshold inherited from P1 F-1.3 for comparability. Substrate-specific σ derivation deferred to P3. Non-blocking at PHASE-READY (C4-tracking). Will resolve at P3 CBT.

verification_protocol: Fracttalix Phase 1-2 Merged, S42, Stage 2.1 (I-9, 7-step) + Step 5b (S44 amendment) + Principle 10 audit (S48 amendment)
falsification_syntax: I-2 5-part (FALSIFIED IF / WHERE / EVALUATION / BOUNDARY / CONTEXT)

inference_rule_inventory.note: IR-1–IR-8 from schema v2-S42. IR-9–IR-11 added for P2 RG derivation content. Addition of IR-9–IR-11 is a Phase 1 finding per HR-1.1 resolution: the IR inventory is not closed at eight rules by fiat — it is closed when sufficient for the content. Each new rule is justified below.

rules.name: Modus Ponens
rules.logical_form: If P and (P → Q), then Q.
rules.p2_example: If a system satisfies class membership criteria (a)–(c) [P], and criteria (a)–(c) imply Ω*=π/2 [P→Q], then the system exhibits Ω*=π/2 [Q].

rules.name: Universal Instantiation
rules.logical_form: If ∀x P(x), then P(a) for any specific a.
rules.p2_example: If all systems in the universality class exhibit β=1/2 [∀x P(x)], then the FRM (a specific system in the class) exhibits β=1/2 [P(a)].

rules.name: Substitution of Equals
rules.logical_form: If a=b, then any expression containing a may replace a with b.
rules.p2_example: If Ω*=π/2 (derived from class criteria), substitute into the critical exponent formula β = Ω*/π to obtain β = (π/2)/π = 1/2.

rules.name: Definition Expansion
rules.logical_form: Replace a defined term with its definition.
rules.p2_example: Expand 'delayed negative feedback network' using the formal definition (criteria a–c) to obtain the explicit DDE form at criticality.

rules.name: Algebraic Manipulation
rules.logical_form: Valid algebraic transformation preserving equality or inequality.
rules.p2_example: From the characteristic equation det(λI − J − Ke^{−λτ}) = 0 at the Hopf point, isolate the imaginary part to obtain Ωτ = π/2 + nπ, take the fundamental mode n=0.

rules.name: Logical Equivalence
rules.logical_form: Replace an expression with a logically equivalent one.
rules.p2_example: 'The scaling dimension of the order parameter at the RG fixed point equals 1/2' is logically equivalent to 'β=1/2' given the definition of β as the order-parameter scaling exponent.

rules.name: Statistical Inference
rules.logical_form: Apply a named statistical test or estimation procedure to data.
rules.p2_example: Not applicable to P2 derivation content. IR-7 applies to C-2.4 substrate verification: confirm β=1/2 for each non-DDE substrate class by applying the standard linear regression protocol (P3 C-3.REG) to the substrate-class dataset.

rules.name: Parsimony / Modeling Principle Selection
rules.logical_form: Select a canonical value from a family of axiom-consistent options based on a named parsimony principle.
rules.p2_example: For the RG flow near the fixed point, multiple consistent truncation orders exist. Select the leading-order truncation on the basis of the canonical parsimony principle: the lowest-order term that captures the critical exponent exactly. Document as canonical choice, not necessary derivation.

rules.name: RG Fixed-Point Identification
rules.logical_form: A fixed point of the RG transformation T is a point x* such that T(x*) = x*. The critical exponent β is determined by the eigenvalue spectrum of dT/dx at x*.
rules.p2_example: Apply the RG transformation to the universality class Lagrangian/equation of motion. Identify the fixed point by solving T(x*) = x*. Compute d(scaling dimension)/d(RG flow parameter) at x* to extract β.
rules.justification: IR-9 added because IR-1–IR-8 do not contain a rule for fixed-point identification in a dynamical system (IR-5 covers algebraic manipulation but not the RG flow structure). The fixed-point identification is a named mathematical procedure from renormalization group theory (Wilson 1971), not a new inference principle — it is a specialisation of the general fixed-point theorem applied in a specific context. IR-9 is therefore a legitimate extension, not a circular definition.

rules.name: Universality Class Identification
rules.logical_form: Two systems belong to the same universality class if and only if they share the same RG fixed point structure (same fixed point and same eigenvalue spectrum at that fixed point).
rules.p2_example: Show that the linearised dynamics of a Kuramoto network near criticality (satisfying criteria a–c) and the linearised FRM DDE near criticality share the same RG fixed point and the same leading eigenvalue (= 1/2). Conclude: both belong to the FRM universality class.
rules.justification: IR-10 added because universality class membership requires a specific logical structure (shared RG fixed point) that cannot be reduced to IR-2 (universal instantiation) without circularity. IR-10 is the standard renormalization group definition of universality (Kadanoff 1966, Wilson 1971).

rules.name: Perturbation Stability Argument
rules.logical_form: A fixed point x* is stable under perturbation ε if all eigenvalues of dT/dx at x* have magnitude < 1 (in RG flow sense: perturbations flow back to x* under repeated application of T).
rules.p2_example: Perturb the class membership criteria by ε (e.g. allow slight deviation from exact Hopf condition). Show that the perturbed RG flow returns to x* = 1/2, and the shift in β is O(ε). This establishes C-2.5 (fixed-point stability).
rules.justification: IR-11 added because stability of the fixed point is a distinct logical step from identifying it (IR-9). Stability requires showing the eigenvalue magnitude condition, which is a separate computation.

inbound_dependencies.content: β=1/2 proved for FRM (DDE) via Hopf quarter-wave theorem. At α=0: cos(Ω)=0 → Ω*=π/2 → β=1/2.
inbound_dependencies.status: LIVE — P1 PHASE-READY, AI layer v11 published on GitHub
inbound_dependencies.note: P2 receives this as established input. P2 must derive β=1/2 independently from the universality class criteria — NOT by re-running F-1.4. F-1.4 is confirmation that the FRM is within the class, not the proof of universality.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: FRM Universality Class — Structural Definition
claim_registry.statement: The FRM universality class is the set of dynamical systems satisfying all three of the following structural criteria: (a) TOPOLOGY: the system exhibits delayed negative feedback with a single dominant delay τ > 0; (b) LINEARISATION: the dynamics near the critical point are governed by a characteristic equation with a conjugate pair of roots crossing the imaginary axis at the Hopf bifurcation; (c) TIMESCALE: τ is independently measurable and not inferred from the oscillation period.
derivation_source: Network topology (criterion a): graph-theoretic property of the feedback architecture
Bifurcation theory (criterion b): standard Hopf condition on the characteristic equation
Measurement axiom (criterion c): upstream measurement principle, consistent with DRS C3-analogue at class level
claim_registry.principle_10_note: Criteria (a)–(c) are structural properties derivable from network topology and bifurcation theory. Criterion (c) is derived from the measurement axiom (no circular inference from the oscillation). NONE of the criteria presuppose β=1/2. This satisfies Principle 10 and HR-2.1 resolution (non-circular class definition).
claim_registry.status: PHASE-1-DRAFT — to be confirmed at Phase 3 Stage 3.1

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: β=1/2 Derivation Validity
claim_registry.statement: β=1/2 is the universal critical exponent for systems in the FRM universality class. This follows necessarily from the RG fixed-point analysis applied to the class definition D-2.1. The derivation is step-indexed and each step is valid under IR-1–IR-11.
claim_registry.status: PREDICATE WRITTEN — Phase 2 complete. Derivation table pending Phase 3.
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: PLACEHOLDER PH-2.VAC-1 — derivation_table_step_1_output; populated at Phase 3 Stage 3.2

WHERE.n_invalid_steps: INTEGER — count of steps in P2 Section 3 derivation table where output does not follow from inputs under named rule IR-X
WHERE.rg_fixed_point_exponent: SCALAR — value of beta produced by RG fixed-point analysis from D-2.1 without importing P1 F-1.4 result
WHERE.class_definition_contains_stipulated_con: BOOLEAN — TRUE if any constant in D-2.1(a)-(c) is absent from principle_10_audit with non-null derivation_path

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Trace P2 Section 3 step by step. For each step: verify output follows from inputs by named IR rule. Record n_invalid_steps. Record final output as rg_fixed_point_exponent. Scan principle_10_audit for null derivation_path entries in D-2.1 constants.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: A step is invalid IFF output does not follow from inputs under named rule as stated. Invalid per se: (1) circular steps assuming beta=1/2; (2) steps introducing undefined variables; (3) steps appealing to unlisted rules. rg_fixed_point_exponent != 0.5 falsifies independently of n_invalid_steps.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.1. Type B-1 derivation validity. Threshold: n_invalid_steps = 0 is the only passing value — logical necessity, not statistical threshold. Principle 10: derivation must be DDE-independent (HR-3.1 — Omega*=pi/2 must follow from D-2.1 without P1 F-1.4). Vacuity witness: PH-2.VAC-1 pending Phase 3.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Universality Class Membership
claim_registry.statement: The FRM belongs to the universality class characterised by β=1/2 and functional form f(t) = B + A·exp(−λt)·cos(ωt+φ). Membership is determined by criteria D-2.1(a)–(c), not by analogy.
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: LIVE: frm_criterion_a_satisfied — P1 AI layer v11 frm_parameters.lambda.expression uses tau_gen as sole delay parameter. PH-2.VAC-2 resolved.

WHERE.frm_fails_any_criterion: BOOLEAN — TRUE if FRM fails D-2.1(a), (b), or (c). Evaluated against P1 AI layer v11 live edge F-1.4
WHERE.membership_criterion_is_circular: BOOLEAN — TRUE if any criterion in D-2.1(a)-(c) uses beta=1/2 as a defining input
WHERE.beta_not_implied_by_criteria: BOOLEAN — TRUE if C-2.1 is FALSIFIED (logically downstream)

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Step 1-3: verify D-2.1(a)(b)(c) against P1 AI layer v11. Step 4: scan D-2.1 text for beta/1/2/exponent in definitional role. Step 5: check C-2.1 verdict. Step 6: evaluate FALSIFIED IF.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: All three criteria jointly necessary. Circular IFF beta=1/2 appears as a defining property. Correct form: structural properties (a)-(c) are stated; beta=1/2 is derived from them by IR-9/IR-10.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.2. Type B-2 membership. Two conditions: (i) FRM satisfies criteria; (ii) criteria are non-circular. Both required. D-2.1 draft passes circularity scan. Principle 10: D-2.1(a)-(c) derivation paths pending Phase 3 Stage 3.1 (P10-GAP-2.2). Vacuity witness LIVE.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Functional Form Universality
claim_registry.statement: Systems in the FRM universality class exhibit f(t) = B + A·exp(−λt)·cos(ωt+φ) with λ and ω determined by the same derived expressions as in P1.
claim_registry.status: PREDICATE WRITTEN — Phase 2 complete. Empirical component pending P3 C-3.REG.

WHERE.system_in_class_exhibits_different_scali: BOOLEAN — TRUE if any D-2.1-satisfying system exhibits different lambda/omega scaling. CROSS-PAPER PLACEHOLDER PH-2.3: pending P3 C-3.REG
WHERE.lambda_omega_expressions_not_universal: BOOLEAN — TRUE if lambda=|alpha|/(Gamma*tau) and omega=pi/(2*tau) do not follow as corollaries of C-2.1 derivation

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each verified substrate class, apply P3 C-3.REG to extract lambda and omega. Compare against derived expressions. Verify expressions appear in C-2.1 derivation table as corollaries.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Gamma = 1+pi^2/4 DERIVED (P1 live edge). tau independently measured (D-2.1(c)). alpha = bifurcation distance (P1 scope_boundary). No stipulated constants.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.3. Extends P1 functional form to all class members. Downstream of C-2.1 (lambda/omega as corollaries) and P3 C-3.REG (empirical). PH-2.3 non-blocking at PHASE-READY.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Substrate Independence (resolves PH-1.1)
claim_registry.statement: The critical exponent β=1/2 holds across all substrates satisfying the class membership criteria D-2.1(a)–(c), not only for DDEs. This upgrades PH-1.1 from placeholder to live claim.
claim_registry.status: PREDICATE WRITTEN — Phase 2 complete. Empirical β fitting pending P3/Phase 3.
claim_registry.resolution_procedure: At P2 CBT execution (Phase 5): additive update to P1 AI layer — replace PH-1.1.resolved=false with PH-1.1.resolved=true, add target_claim=C-2.4. Thomas sign-off required. P1 CBT Step 7 re-run after.

WHERE.any_substrate_class_mean_beta_outside_2s: BOOLEAN — TRUE if mean fitted beta for any D-2.1-satisfying substrate class lies outside 0.5 +/- 2*sigma_S. Classes: (1) Kuramoto networks; (2) gene regulatory networks; (3) neural circuits with delayed inhibition. PLACEHOLDER PH-2.4-EMPIRICAL: pending P3 C-3.REG

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Step 1: verify D-2.1(a)-(c) for each substrate class (Phase 3 Stage 3.3). Step 2: apply C-3.REG to extract beta per system. Step 3: compute mean and sigma per class. Step 4: check 2*sigma bound.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: 2-sigma threshold: inherited from P1 F-1.3 for comparability. Limitation registered as P10-GAP-2.5 — substrate-specific sigma deferred to P3. A system failing D-2.1 is not a counterexample. The only counterexample is D-2.1-satisfying AND mean beta outside 2*sigma.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.4 resolves PH-1.1. At P2 CBT: P1 AI layer updated (additive — PH-1.1.resolved=true, target_claim=C-2.4). Thomas sign-off required. P1 CBT Step 7 re-run. Analytic component established by C-2.1+C-2.2; empirical pending P3.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: RG Fixed-Point Stability
claim_registry.statement: The β=1/2 fixed point is stable under perturbation of the class membership conditions. Shift in β is O(ε) for perturbation of size ε.
claim_registry.status: PREDICATE WRITTEN — Phase 2 complete. Stability computation pending Phase 3 Stage 3.2.

WHERE.beta_shift_exceeds_order_epsilon: BOOLEAN — TRUE if |beta(epsilon) - 0.5| grows faster than linearly in epsilon as epsilon->0
WHERE.fixed_point_eigenvalue_exceeds_unity: BOOLEAN — TRUE if any eigenvalue of dT/dx at x*=0.5 has magnitude >= 1

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: From derivation table: locate T and x*=0.5. Compute dT/dx at x*. Find eigenvalues. Perturb D-2.1 by epsilon, compute beta(epsilon), evaluate Δβ/ε as ε->0.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: ALL eigenvalues must be < 1. Single eigenvalue >= 1 falsifies. O(ε) bound: Lipschitz continuity of RG flow — mathematical axiom, not stipulated (registered in principle_10_audit).
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.5. Stability makes universality physically meaningful — class is not measure-zero. Without stability, β=1/2 holds only for fine-tuned systems. Standard RG stability criterion (IR-11).

mandatory_checks: Step 1: Schema compliance (schema_version v2-S42+S48, paper_type derivation_B, principle_10_compliant field present, principle_10_audit array present)

Step 2: Predicate completeness (5-part syntax FALSIFIED IF / WHERE / EVALUATION / BOUNDARY / CONTEXT for all C-2.1–C-2.5, threshold justification in CONTEXT, vacuity_witness populated — not placeholder)
Step 3: Derivation table validation (Type B specific — each step names IR rule, output follows from inputs, n_invalid_steps = 0, no circular steps)
Step 4: Principle 10 audit (all principle_10_audit entries have derivation_path anchored at published live edges or IR axioms — circularity detection per S48-A2; no gap_id entries remaining)
Step 5: Dependency closure (C4 mode: PHASE-READY-TRACKING — all outbound edges have placeholder_register entries in correct format)
Step 5b: WHERE field external reference scan (I9-S44-A1 — all strings matching P[N]/Paper[N]/Appendix[X] in WHERE fields of C-2.1–C-2.5 have corresponding placeholder_register entries)
Step 6: Cross-corpus placeholder scan (PROC-GRAPH v2 notation — no cross-corpus references expected in P2 at this stage)
Step 7: Holistic coherence review (all prose claims have AI layer entries, abstract in DRS Section 2 template format, section structure mirrors claim order)

thomas_review_flags.description: RG flow from substrate-general G(iΩ,τ) to scalar exponential fixed-point form. Argument basis: Coullet & Spiegel (1983) amplitude equations — leading-order delayed feedback operator survives RG flow. Physically sound; mathematical physics level. Thomas to review before journal submission.

Eigenvalue normalisation for RG stability. Resolution: stability is of the amplitude direction (exp(−λ·τ_RG) < 1 for λ>0), not the phase angle (which gives eigenvalue π/2 in unnormalised units). Thomas to verify the amplitude vs phase direction argument is correctly stated for publication.
